value.hq: Multiple: Miami, Florida (current corporate HQ) and Oxford, UK / Menlo Park, CA / Princeton, NJ / Dublin, Ireland offices; originally headquartered in Abingdon/Oxford, UK

value.founding_story: Founded in 2003 in Oxford, UK as an Anglo-American antibiotics and rare-disease developer, originally listed on London's AIM market as Summit Corporation plc/Summit Therapeutics plc. Pursued a C. difficile antibiotic (ridinilazole) and a Duchenne muscular dystrophy utrophin-modulator program (ezutromid/SMT C1100) as its founding pipeline.

value.total_raised: unconfirmed aggregate — individually disclosed raises total at least ~$824M across the 2015 IPO, 2020 Duggan private placement, 2023 rights offering and 2024 registered direct (see rounds)

value.indication: Non-small cell lung cancer and other solid tumors (ivonescimab, PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody)
value.phase: Phase III (multiple studies: HARMONi, HARMONi-3, HARMONi-7, HARMONi-GI3); BLA under FDA review
value.status: active, BLA accepted with PDUFA date November 2026

value.royalty: tiered royalties (rate not disclosed in sources reviewed)

value.milestones: up to $4,500,000,000 in regulatory and commercial milestones (total deal value up to $5.0 billion)
value.royalty: low double-digit percentage of net sales
value.territory: United States, Canada, Europe, Japan (Akeso retains China and rest of world)

value.milestones: up to $104,500,000 in regulatory and commercial milestones

value.name: Robert W. Duggan
prior: Chairman & CEO, Pharmacyclics, Inc. (sold to AbbVie, 2015)

value.from: Duchenne muscular dystrophy utrophin modulation (ezutromid/SMT C1100), a founding-era pipeline pillar carried through a 2016 European out-license to Sarepta
value.to: DMD program fully discontinued; Summit narrowed to anti-infectives (ridinilazole)
value.trigger: PhaseOut DMD Phase 2 trial failed to meet primary or secondary endpoints, announced 2018-06-27
value.outcome: no utrophin-modulator or DMD asset has re-entered Summit's pipeline through 2026; company narrowed to ridinilazole as sole clinical asset until the 2022 Akeso deal

value.from: ridinilazole as Summit's lead late-stage clinical asset and core anti-infectives focus, the company's identity since its 2003 founding
value.to: immuno-oncology via in-licensed ivonescimab, with ridinilazole progressively deprioritized and ultimately divested entirely to Biossil, Inc. on 2026-07-14
value.trigger: Ri-CoDIFy Phase III trial for ridinilazole missed its primary superiority endpoint, announced 2021-12-20 (49% single-day stock decline)
value.outcome: as of 2026-07-20, Summit's entire clinical pipeline is ivonescimab-centric (HARMONi program); ridinilazole fully exited via the Biossil sale six days before this scan's as_of date

value.detail: PhaseOut DMD Phase 2 trial of ezutromid failed to meet primary or secondary endpoints; Summit discontinued ezutromid development and announced cost-reduction measures, with stock falling approximately 80% on the news.

Phase III Ri-CoDIFy study of ridinilazole missed its primary superiority endpoint versus vancomycin (though it showed reduced recurrence, nominal p=0.0002); the asset would have required at least one additional Phase III trial before regulatory submission. Stock fell approximately 49% same-day.
Summit signed away its remaining rights to ridinilazole entirely, selling the asset to Biossil, Inc. for a nominal $500,000 upfront, ending the company's 20+ year anti-infectives history.
